name: Housing Units Occupancy
description: This layer shows housing occupancy, tenure, and median rent/housing value. This is shown by tract, county, and state boundaries. This service is updated annually to contain the most currently released American Community Survey (ACS) 5-year data, and contains estimates and margins of error. There are also additional calculated attributes related to this topic, which can be mapped or used within analysis. Homeownership rate on Census Bureau's website is owner-occupied housing unit rate (called B25003_calc_pctOwnE in this layer). <div><br /></div><div>This layer is symbolized by the overall homeownership rate. To see the full list of attributes available in this service, go to the &quot;Data&quot; tab, and choose &quot;Fields&quot; at the top right. <div><br /></div><div><div><b>Current Vintage</b>: <span style="font-family:&quot;Avenir Next W01&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next W00&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next&quot;, Avenir, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size:16px;">2019-2023</span></div><div><b>ACS Table(s)</b>: B25002, B25003, B25058, B25077, B25057, B25059, B25076, B25078</div><div><b>Data downloaded from</b>: <a href="https://www.census.gov/data/developers/data-sets.html" target="_blank">Census Bureau's API for American Community Survey</a> </div><div><b><br /></b></div><div><b>The United States Census Bureau's American Community Survey (ACS):</b></div><div><ul><li><a href="https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/about.html" target="_blank">About the Survey</a><br /></li><li><a href="https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/geography-acs.html" target="_blank">Geography &amp; ACS</a><br /></li><li><a href="https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/technical-documentation.html" target="_blank">Technical Documentation</a><br /></li><li><a href="https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/news.html" target="_blank">News &amp; Updates</a><br /></li></ul><div>This ready-to-use layer can be used within ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, its configurable apps, dashboards, Story Maps, custom apps, and mobile apps. Data can also be exported for offline workflows. For more information about ACS layers, visit the <a href="https://acs-hosted-feature-layers-faq-esri.hub.arcgis.com/" target="_blank">FAQ</a>. Please cite the Census and ACS when using this data.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><div><b>Data Note from the Census:</b><br /></div><div>Data are based on a sample and are subject to sampling variability. The degree of uncertainty for an estimate arising from sampling variability is represented through the use of a margin of error. The value shown here is the 90 percent margin of error. The margin of error can be interpreted as providing a 90 percent probability that the interval defined by the estimate minus the margin of error and the estimate plus the margin of error (the lower and upper confidence bounds) contains the true value. In addition to sampling variability, the ACS estimates are subject to nonsampling error (for a discussion of nonsampling variability, see <a href="https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/technical-documentation/code-lists.html" target="_blank">Accuracy of the Data</a>).  The effect of nonsampling error is not represented in these tables.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><b>Data Processing Notes:</b></div><div><ul><li>This layer is updated automatically when the most current vintage of ACS data is released each year, usually in December. The layer always contains the latest available ACS 5-year estimates. It is updated annually within days of the Census Bureau's release schedule. <a href="https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/news/data-releases.html" target="_blank">Click here</a> to learn more about ACS data releases.<br /></li><li>Boundaries come from the <a href="https://www.census.gov/geographies/mapping-files/time-series/geo/tiger-geodatabase-file.html" style="color:rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line:none; font-family:inherit;">US Census TIGER geodatabases,</a> specifically, the National Sub-State Geography Database (named tlgdb_(year)_a_us_substategeo.gdb). Boundaries are updated at the same time as the data updates (annually), and the boundary vintage appropriately matches the data vintage <a href="https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/geography-acs/geography-boundaries-by-year.html" style="color:rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line:none; font-family:inherit;">as specified by the Census</a>. These are Census boundaries with water and/or coastlines erased for cartographic and mapping purposes. For census tracts, the water cutouts are derived from a subset of the <a href="https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/TGRGDB20/tlgdb_2020_a_us_areawater.gdb.zip" style="color:rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line:none; font-family:inherit;" target="_blank">2020 Areal Hydrography boundaries offered by TIGER</a>. Water bodies and rivers which are 50 million square meters or larger (mid to large sized water bodies) are erased from the tract level boundaries, as well as additional important features. For state and county boundaries, the water and coastlines are derived from the coastlines of the 2022 500k <a href="https://www.census.gov/geographies/mapping-files/time-series/geo/cartographic-boundary.html" style="color:rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line:none; font-family:inherit;">TIGER Cartographic Boundary Shapefiles</a>. These are erased to more accurately portray the coastlines and Great Lakes. The original AWATER and ALAND fields are still available as attributes within the data table (units are square meters).</li><li>The States layer contains 52 records - all US states, Washington D.C., and Puerto Rico</li><li>Census tracts with no population that occur in areas of water, such as oceans, are removed from this data service (Census Tracts beginning with 99).</li><li>Percentages and derived counts, and associated margins of error, are calculated values (that can be identified by the &quot;_calc_&quot; stub in the field name), and abide by the specifications <a href="https://www.census.gov/content/dam/Census/library/publications/2020/acs/acs_general_handbook_2020_ch08.pdf" style="color:rgb(0, 121, 193); text-decoration-line:none; font-family:inherit;" target="_blank">defined by the American Community Survey</a>.</li><li>Field alias names were created based on the Table Shells file available from the <a href="https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/technical-documentation/table-shells.html" target="_blank">American Community Survey Summary File Documentation page</a>.<br /></li><li>Negative values (e.g., -4444...) have been set to null, with the exception of -5555... which has been set to zero. These negative values exist in the raw API data to indicate the following situations:</li><ul><li>The margin of error column indicates that either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute a standard error and thus the margin of error. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>Either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute an estimate, or a ratio of medians cannot be calculated because one or both of the median estimates falls in the lowest interval or upper interval of an open-ended distribution.<br /></li><li>The median falls in the lowest interval of an open-ended distribution, or in the upper interval of an open-ended distribution. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>The estimate is controlled. A statistical test for sampling variability is not appropriate.<br /></li><li>The data for this geographic area cannot be displayed because the number of sample cases is too small.</li></ul></ul></div></div></div>

fields.name: OBJECTID
fields.alias: Object ID
fields.description: {"value":"An ObjectID is a unique, not null integer field used to uniquely identify rows in tables in a geographical table.", "fieldValueType":"uniqueIdentifier"}

fields.name: Shape__Area
fields.alias: Shape__Area

fields.name: Shape__Length
fields.alias: Shape__Length

fields.name: GEOID
fields.alias: Geographic Identifier - FIPS Code
fields.description: {"value":"Geographic Identifier, or GEOID is the numeric code used to uniquely identify all administrative/legal and statistical geographic areas for which the Census Bureau tabulates data.", "fieldValueType":"uniqueIdentifier"}

fields.name: ALAND
fields.alias: Area of Land (Square Meters)
fields.description: {"value":"Current area of land in square meters within this geographical area", "fieldValueType":"measurement"}

fields.name: AWATER
fields.alias: Area of Water (Square Meters)
fields.description: {"value":"Current area of water in square meters within this geographical area", "fieldValueType":"measurement"}

fields.name: NAME
fields.alias: Name
fields.description: {"value":"Name of this geographical area", "fieldValueType":"nameOrTitle"}

fields.name: State
fields.alias: State
fields.description: {"value":"State in which this geography resides", "fieldValueType":"nameOrTitle"}

fields.name: County
fields.alias: County
fields.description: {"value":"County in which this geography resides", "fieldValueType":"nameOrTitle"}

fields.name: B25002_001E
fields.alias: Total Housing Units
fields.description: {"value":"A housing unit may be a house, an apartment, a mobile home, or rooms which have direct access from outside the building or through a common hall. Boats, recreational vehicles (RVs), vans, tents, railroad cars, and the like are included only if they are occupied as someone's current place of residence. ", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25002_001M
fields.alias: Total Housing Units - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Total Housing Units (90% confidence). A housing unit may be a house, an apartment, a mobile home, or rooms which have direct access from outside the building or through a common hall. Boats, recreational vehicles (RVs), vans, tents, railroad cars, and the like are included only if they are occupied as someone's current place of residence. ", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25002_002E
fields.alias: Occupied Housing Units
fields.description: {"value":"A housing unit is classified as occupied if it is the current place of residence of the person or group of people living in it at the time of interview, or if the occupants are only temporarily absent from the residence for two months or less, that is, away on vacation or a business trip.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25002_002M
fields.alias: Occupied Housing Units - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Occupied Housing Units (90% confidence). A housing unit is classified as occupied if it is the current place of residence of the person or group of people living in it at the time of interview, or if the occupants are only temporarily absent from the residence for two months or less, that is, away on vacation or a business trip.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25002_003E
fields.alias: Vacant Housing Units
fields.description: {"value":"A housing unit is vacant if no one is living in it at the time of interview. Units occupied at the time of interview entirely by persons who are staying two months or less and who have a more permanent residence elsewhere are considered to be temporarily occupied, and are classified as \"vacant.\" ", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25002_003M
fields.alias: Vacant Housing Units - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Vacant Housing Units (90% confidence). A housing unit is vacant if no one is living in it at the time of interview. Units occupied at the time of interview entirely by persons who are staying two months or less and who have a more permanent residence elsewhere are considered to be temporarily occupied, and are classified as \"vacant.\"", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25002_calc_pctVacE
fields.alias: Percent of Housing Units that are Vacant (Vacancy Rate)
fields.description: {"value":"A housing unit is vacant if no one is living in it at the time of interview. Units occupied at the time of interview entirely by persons who are staying two months or less and who have a more permanent residence elsewhere are considered to be temporarily occupied, and are classified as \"vacant.\" Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B25002_003E  /  B25002_001E , 1 )", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25002_calc_pctVacM
fields.alias: Percent of Housing Units that are Vacant (Vacancy Rate) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Percent of Housing Units that are Vacant (Vacancy Rate). A housing unit is vacant if no one is living in it at the time of interview. Units occupied at the time of interview entirely by persons who are staying two months or less and who have a more permanent residence elsewhere are considered to be temporarily occupied, and are classified as \"vacant.\" Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B25002_001E)*(sqrt((B25002_003M)^2-1.0*((B25002_003E  /  B25002_001E)^2)*(B25002_001M)^2))", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25002_calc_pctTotalOwnE
fields.alias: Percent of Total Housing Units (including Vacant) that are Owner-Occupied
fields.description: {"value":"A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B25003_002E  /  B25002_001E , 1)", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25002_calc_pctTotalOwnM
fields.alias: Percent of Total Housing Units (including Vacant) that are Owner-Occupied - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Percent of Total Housing Units (including Vacant) that are Owner-Occupied (90% confidence). A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B25002_001E)*(sqrt((B25002_002M)^2-1.0*((B25003_002E  /  B25002_001E)^2)*(B25002_001M)^2))", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25002_calc_pctTotalRentE
fields.alias: Percent of Total Housing Units (including Vacant) that are Renter-Occupied
fields.description: {"value":"All occupied housing units which are not owner-occupied, whether they are rented or occupied without payment of rent, are classified as renter-occupied. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B25003_003E  /  B25002_001E , 1)", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25002_calc_pctTotalRentM
fields.alias: Percent of Total Housing Units (including Vacant) that are Renter-Occupied - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Percent of Total Housing Units (including Vacant) that are Renter-Occupied (90% confidence). Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B25002_001E)*(sqrt((B25003_003M)^2-1.0*((B25003_003E  /  B25002_001E)^2)*(B25002_001M)^2))", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25003_001E
fields.alias: Total Occupied Housing Units
fields.description: {"value":"A housing unit is classified as occupied if it is the current place of residence of the person or group of people living in it at the time of interview, or if the occupants are only temporarily absent from the residence for two months or less, that is, away on vacation or a business trip.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25003_001M
fields.alias: Total Occupied Housing Units - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Total Occupied Housing Units (90% confidence). A housing unit is classified as occupied if it is the current place of residence of the person or group of people living in it at the time of interview, or if the occupants are only temporarily absent from the residence for two months or less, that is, away on vacation or a business trip.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25003_002E
fields.alias: Owner-Occupied Housing Units
fields.description: {"value":"A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25003_002M
fields.alias: Owner-Occupied Housing Units - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Owner-Occupied Housing Units (90% confidence). A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25003_003E
fields.alias: Renter-Occupied Housing Units
fields.description: {"value":"All occupied housing units which are not owner-occupied, whether they are rented or occupied without payment of rent, are classified as renter-occupied.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25003_003M
fields.alias: Renter-Occupied Housing Units - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Renter-Occupied Housing Units (90% confidence). All occupied housing units which are not owner-occupied, whether they are rented or occupied without payment of rent, are classified as renter-occupied.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25003_calc_pctOwnE
fields.alias: Overall Homeownership Rate: Percent of Occupied Housing Units that are Owner-Occupied
fields.description: {"value":"A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B25003_002E  /  B25003_001E , 1)", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25003_calc_pctOwnM
fields.alias: Overall Homeownership Rate: Percent of Occupied Housing Units that are Owner-Occupied - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Overall Homeownership Rate: Percent of Occupied Housing Units that are Owner-Occupied (90% confidence). A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B25003_001E)*(sqrt((B25003_002M)^2-1.0*((B25003_002E  /  B25003_001E)^2)*(B25003_001M)^2))", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25003_calc_pctRentE
fields.alias: Overall Renter Rate: Percent of Occupied Housing Units that are Renter-Occupied
fields.description: {"value":"All occupied housing units which are not owner-occupied, whether they are rented or occupied without payment of rent, are classified as renter-occupied. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B25003_003E  /  B25003_001E, 1)", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25003_calc_pctRentM
fields.alias: Overall Renter Rate: Percent of Occupied Housing Units that are Renter-Occupied - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Overall Renter Rate: Percent of Occupied Housing Units that are Renter-Occupied (90% confidence). All occupied housing units which are not owner-occupied, whether they are rented or occupied without payment of rent, are classified as renter-occupied. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B25003_001E)*(sqrt((B25003_003M)^2-1.0*((B25003_003E  /  B25003_001E)^2)*(B25003_001M)^2))", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B25058_001E
fields.alias: Median Contract Rent (Contract Rent for Renter-Occupied Housing Units Paying Cash Rent)
fields.description: {"value":"Half of rented units have a higher contract rent than the median contract rent and half of rented units have a lower contract rent. Contract rent is the monthly rent agreed to or contracted for, regardless of any furnishings, utilities, fees, meals, or services that may be included. Only renter-occupied housing units paying cash rent are included. Units not paying cash rent are generally provided free by friends or relatives or in exchange for services such as resident manager, caretaker, minister, or tenant farmer; or housing on military bases.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25058_001M
fields.alias: Median Contract Rent (Contract Rent for Renter-Occupied Housing Units Paying Cash Rent) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Median Contract Rent (Contract Rent for Renter-Occupied Housing Units Paying Cash Rent) (90% confidence). Half of rented units have a higher contract rent than the median contract rent and half of rented units have a lower contract rent. Contract rent is the monthly rent agreed to or contracted for, regardless of any furnishings, utilities, fees, meals, or services that may be included. Only renter-occupied housing units paying cash rent are included. Units not paying cash rent are generally provided free by friends or relatives or in exchange for services such as resident manager, caretaker, minister, or tenant farmer; or housing on military bases.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25077_001E
fields.alias: Median Home Value (for Owner-Occupied Housing Units)
fields.description: {"value":"Half of homes have a higher value than the median home value and half of homes have a lower value. Value is the respondent's estimate of how much the property (house and lot, mobile home and lot (if lot owned), or condominium unit) would sell for if it were for sale. Only owner-occupied housing units included (A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for).", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25077_001M
fields.alias: Median Home Value (for Owner-Occupied Housing Units) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Median Home Value (for Owner-Occupied Housing Units) (90% confidence). Half of homes have a higher value than the median home value and half of homes have a lower value. Value is the respondent's estimate of how much the property (house and lot, mobile home and lot (if lot owned), or condominium unit) would sell for if it were for sale. Only owner-occupied housing units included (A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for).", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25057_001E
fields.alias: Lower Contract Rent Quartile (Contract Rent for Renter-Occupied Housing Units Paying Cash Rent)
fields.description: {"value":"75 percent of rented units have a higher contract rent than the lower contract rent quartile and 25 percent of rented units have a higher contract rent. Contract rent is the monthly rent agreed to or contracted for, regardless of any furnishings, utilities, fees, meals, or services that may be included. Only renter-occupied housing units paying cash rent are included. Units not paying cash rent are generally provided free by friends or relatives or in exchange for services such as resident manager, caretaker, minister, or tenant farmer; or housing on military bases.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25057_001M
fields.alias: Lower Contract Rent Quartile (Contract Rent for Renter-Occupied Housing Units Paying Cash Rent) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Lower Contract Rent Quartile (Contract Rent for Renter-Occupied Housing Units Paying Cash Rent) (90% confidence). 75 percent of rented units have a higher contract rent than the lower contract rent quartile and 25 percent of rented units have a higher contract rent. Contract rent is the monthly rent agreed to or contracted for, regardless of any furnishings, utilities, fees, meals, or services that may be included. Only renter-occupied housing units paying cash rent are included. Units not paying cash rent are generally provided free by friends or relatives or in exchange for services such as resident manager, caretaker, minister, or tenant farmer; or housing on military bases.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25059_001E
fields.alias: Upper Contract Rent Quartile (Contract Rent for Renter-Occupied Housing Units Paying Cash Rent)
fields.description: {"value":"25 percent of rented units have a higher contract rent than the upper contract rent quartile and 75 percent of rented units have a higher contract rent. Contract rent is the monthly rent agreed to or contracted for, regardless of any furnishings, utilities, fees, meals, or services that may be included. Only renter-occupied housing units paying cash rent are included. Units not paying cash rent are generally provided free by friends or relatives or in exchange for services such as resident manager, caretaker, minister, or tenant farmer; or housing on military bases.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25059_001M
fields.alias: Upper Contract Rent Quartile (Contract Rent for Renter-Occupied Housing Units Paying Cash Rent) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Upper Contract Rent Quartile (Contract Rent for Renter-Occupied Housing Units Paying Cash Rent) (90% confidence). 25 percent of rented units have a higher contract rent than the upper contract rent quartile and 75 percent of rented units have a higher contract rent. Contract rent is the monthly rent agreed to or contracted for, regardless of any furnishings, utilities, fees, meals, or services that may be included. Only renter-occupied housing units paying cash rent are included. Units not paying cash rent are generally provided free by friends or relatives or in exchange for services such as resident manager, caretaker, minister, or tenant farmer; or housing on military bases.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25076_001E
fields.alias: Lower Home Value Quartile (for Owner-Occupied Housing Units)
fields.description: {"value":"75 percent of homes have a higher value than the lower value quartile and 25 percent of homes have a lower value. Value is the respondent's estimate of how much the property (house and lot, mobile home and lot (if lot owned), or condominium unit) would sell for if it were for sale. Only owner-occupied housing units included (A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for).", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25076_001M
fields.alias: Lower Home Value Quartile (for Owner-Occupied Housing Units) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Lower Home Value Quartile (for Owner-Occupied Housing Units) (90% confidence). 75 percent of homes have a higher value than the lower value quartile and 25 percent of homes have a lower value. Value is the respondent's estimate of how much the property (house and lot, mobile home and lot (if lot owned), or condominium unit) would sell for if it were for sale. Only owner-occupied housing units included (A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for).", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25078_001E
fields.alias: Upper Home Value Quartile (for Owner-Occupied Housing Units)
fields.description: {"value":"25 percent of homes have a higher value than the upper home value quartile and 75 percent of homes have a lower value. Value is the respondent's estimate of how much the property (house and lot, mobile home and lot (if lot owned), or condominium unit) would sell for if it were for sale. Only owner-occupied housing units included (A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for).", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B25078_001M
fields.alias: Upper Home Value Quartile (for Owner-Occupied Housing Units) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Upper Home Value Quartile (for Owner-Occupied Housing Units) (90% confidence). 25 percent of homes have a higher value than the upper home value quartile and 75 percent of homes have a lower value. Value is the respondent's estimate of how much the property (house and lot, mobile home and lot (if lot owned), or condominium unit) would sell for if it were for sale. Only owner-occupied housing units included (A housing unit is owner-occupied if the owner or co-owner lives in the unit, even if it is mortgaged or not fully paid for).", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: Year
fields.alias: Year

fields.alias: Place FIPS
fields.description: {"value":"The FIPS Code corresponding to the census designated place in which each census tract resides.","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: WARD
fields.alias: WARD

fields.name: NEIGHBORHOOD
fields.alias: NEIGHBORHOOD
